tossups.question: An indie filmmaker from this state cast Joshua Burge as the lead of his underground black comedies Ape, Buzzard, Relaxer, and Vulcanizadora. In a 1989 documentary set in this home state of Joel Potrykus, rich people pay unemployed people to be human statues for a Great Gatsby-themed party. The set of a 1978 film about workers in this state was plagued by fights among its three stars: Yaphet Kotto, Harvey Keitel, and Richard Pryor. This state is the setting of Blue Collar, the first film directed by its native son Paul Schrader. A 1959 film set in this state stars the (*) lawyer Joseph N. Welch as a judge and has a jazz score by Duke Ellington. An Italian-American flautist named Carmine gave his son born in this state a middle name to honor his employer. A criminal trial in this state forms the plot of Otto Preminger’s drama Anatomy of a Murder. For 10 points, Michael Moore’s film Roger & Me is about what state’s workers being laid off by General Motors in Flint?
tossups.question_sanitized: An indie filmmaker from this state cast Joshua Burge as the lead of his underground black comedies Ape, Buzzard, Relaxer, and Vulcanizadora. In a 1989 documentary set in this home state of Joel Potrykus, rich people pay unemployed people to be human statues for a Great Gatsby-themed party. The set of a 1978 film about workers in this state was plagued by fights among its three stars: Yaphet Kotto, Harvey Keitel, and Richard Pryor. This state is the setting of Blue Collar, the first film directed by its native son Paul Schrader. A 1959 film set in this state stars the (*) lawyer Joseph N. Welch as a judge and has a jazz score by Duke Ellington. An Italian-American flautist named Carmine gave his son born in this state a middle name to honor his employer. A criminal trial in this state forms the plot of Otto Preminger's drama Anatomy of a Murder. For 10 points, Michael Moore's film Roger & Me is about what state's workers being laid off by General Motors in Flint?
tossups.answer: Michigan [or MI] (Carmine Coppola was working for Ford when Francis Ford Coppola was born.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Michigan [or MI] (Carmine Coppola was working for Ford when Francis Ford Coppola was born.)

tossups.question: In this film, the banal discussion of two medics is heard over shots of screens depicting the protagonist’s body violently contorting. Lines from Richard Nixon speeches were used for some dialogue of a character in this film who hoards prism-shaped biscuits. Each time they open a medicine cabinet, this film’s characters hear a voice asking them questions like “are you now, or have you ever been?” and “what’s wrong?” This film’s making-of documentary depicts its female lead crying as she has her head shaved. In this film, a voice calmly stating “Buy more. Buy more now. Buy.” is heard over shots of a Hans Memling portrait of (*) Christ. Sound designer Walter Murch co-wrote this film, whose protagonist drives a jet-powered car to escape police with metal faces. This second American Zoetrope film was based on its director’s USC student film Electronic Labyrinth. For 10 points, the audio company that uses the Deep Note is named after what debut sci-fi film by George Lucas?
tossups.question_sanitized: In this film, the banal discussion of two medics is heard over shots of screens depicting the protagonist's body violently contorting. Lines from Richard Nixon speeches were used for some dialogue of a character in this film who hoards prism-shaped biscuits. Each time they open a medicine cabinet, this film's characters hear a voice asking them questions like "are you now, or have you ever been?" and "what's wrong?" This film's making-of documentary depicts its female lead crying as she has her head shaved. In this film, a voice calmly stating "Buy more. Buy more now. Buy." is heard over shots of a Hans Memling portrait of (*) Christ. Sound designer Walter Murch co-wrote this film, whose protagonist drives a jet-powered car to escape police with metal faces. This second American Zoetrope film was based on its director's USC student film Electronic Labyrinth. For 10 points, the audio company that uses the Deep Note is named after what debut sci-fi film by George Lucas?
tossups.answer: THX 1138 [prompt on THX] (George Lucas has confirmed that for some reason, executive producer Francis Ford Coppola set the film’s budget at exactly $777,777, but I couldn’t find any source for why he did this.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: THX 1138 [prompt on THX] (George Lucas has confirmed that for some reason, executive producer Francis Ford Coppola set the film's budget at exactly $777,777, but I couldn't find any source for why he did this.)

tossups.question: In a film set in this city, a character who admits to stealing newspapers at a confessional insists on wearing a translucent plastic raincoat. At the end of that film set in this city, the protagonist resignedly plays a saxophone in his ruined apartment. Chris Marker’s film La Jetée references a scene of two lovers counting tree rings in a forest near this city. A film set in this city, inspired by Michelangelo Antonioni’s Blow-Up, opens with a (*) descending crane shot onto its central square. The dolly zoom effect was popularized by a film set in this city, whose opening sequence features a spiral imposed on a close-up of an eye. In that film set in this city, the deceased Madeleine Elster is impersonated by Judy, who dies after falling from a bell tower during a rendezvous with the detective Scottie. For 10 points, Alfred Hitchcock’s Vertigo is set in what Bay Area city?
tossups.answer: San Francisco, California [accept San Fran] (the other film is Francis Ford Coppola’s The Conversation.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: San Francisco, California [accept San Fran] (the other film is Francis Ford Coppola's The Conversation.)

tossups.question: A desire to shoot the film entirely underwater led this director to be dropped from a live-action remake of The Little Mermaid. At the end of a film by this director, two characters whisper an inaudible goodbye to each other on a crowded street as the song “Just Like Honey” starts to play. In a film by this director, Cecilia, who leaps onto a fencepost, is the first of the Lisbon sisters to kill herself. A film by this director ends with “More Than This” being performed by the actor Bob Harris, who meets Scarlett Johansson’s character in Japan. This director appeared as the infant Michael Rizzi in a christening scene in a film directed by her father. For 10 points, name this director of The Virgin Suicides and Lost in Translation, whose father directed The Godfather.
tossups.answer: Sofia Coppola [prompt on Coppola; do not accept or prompt on “Francis Ford Coppola”]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Sofia Coppola [prompt on Coppola; do not accept or prompt on "Francis Ford Coppola"]

tossups.question: A film by this director begins with a three-minute slow zoom from an establishing shot of Union Park into a tighter framing of a mime following the protagonist, who later obsesses over the phrase “he’d kill us if he got the chance,” recorded during his job as an audio surveillance expert. This frequent collaborator with sound designer Walter Murch opened another film with a mortician telling the title character “I believe in America.” In that film by this director, a character shoots Officer McCluskey and Sollozzo with a gun retrieved from a restaurant bathroom. This director of The Conversation adapted a Mario Puzo novel into a film in which Marlon Brando’s character says “I’m going to make him an offer he can’t refuse.” For 10 points, name this director of the Godfather trilogy.
tossups.question_sanitized: A film by this director begins with a three-minute slow zoom from an establishing shot of Union Park into a tighter framing of a mime following the protagonist, who later obsesses over the phrase "he'd kill us if he got the chance," recorded during his job as an audio surveillance expert. This frequent collaborator with sound designer Walter Murch opened another film with a mortician telling the title character "I believe in America." In that film by this director, a character shoots Officer McCluskey and Sollozzo with a gun retrieved from a restaurant bathroom. This director of The Conversation adapted a Mario Puzo novel into a film in which Marlon Brando's character says "I'm going to make him an offer he can't refuse." For 10 points, name this director of the Godfather trilogy.

bonuses.leadin: The plural of this word titles Michael Ondaatje’s book of interviews with Walter Murch inspired by Murch’s time working on The English Patient, the first digitally edited film to win the Oscar for Best Editing. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The plural of this word titles Michael Ondaatje's book of interviews with Walter Murch inspired by Murch's time working on The English Patient, the first digitally edited film to win the Oscar for Best Editing. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this word that titles a film which ends as a saxophone-playing surveillance expert rips up his entire apartment looking for a bug after receiving a recording of his playing over the phone.

Murch frequently worked with this audiophile director of The Conversation. Nino Rota scored a film by this director that ends as a door closes in front of a woman watching other men kiss her husband’s hand.
Murch became the first credited Sound Designer for this Coppola film. Ride of the Valkyries plays during a napalm strike in this adaptation of Heart of Darkness set during the Vietnam War.

answers_sanitized: conversation [accept The Conversations; accept The Conversation]

bonuses.leadin: Name some things about the history of women in film, for 10 points each.
parts: This woman won an Academy Award for the screenplay of a film in which the French actress “Her” and the Japanese architect “Him” separate in the wake of World War II, Hiroshima mon amour.

Anita Loos wrote a novel of this name and adapted it into a 1928 screenplay. Its more famous 1953 remake features Marilyn Monroe as the gold-digger Lorelei Lee, who sings “Diamonds Are a Girl’s Best Friend.”

This is the last name of Sofia, who directed a film in which the middle-aged Bob meets the young Charlotte in Japan, Lost in Translation, and had a small role as the baptized baby in her father Francis Ford’s film The Godfather.
answers_sanitized: Coppola [accept Sofia Coppola or Francis Ford Coppola]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Name some things about the history of women in film, for 10 points each.

Anita Loos wrote a novel of this name and adapted it into a 1928 screenplay. Its more famous 1953 remake features Marilyn Monroe as the gold-digger Lorelei Lee, who sings "Diamonds Are a Girl's Best Friend."

This is the last name of Sofia, who directed a film in which the middle-aged Bob meets the young Charlotte in Japan, Lost in Translation, and had a small role as the baptized baby in her father Francis Ford's film The Godfather.

bonuses.leadin: Olivia de Havilland was the first female president of this festival. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this French film festival, which since 1955, has given out the prestigious Palme d'Or to films judged the best.
The controversial winner of the Palme d'Or in 1994 was Pulp Fiction, a movie by this director of such stylish, violent films as Django Unchained, Kill Bill, and Reservoir Dogs.
A two-time winner of the Palme d'Or was this director, who won in 1974 for his film The Conversation. The documentary Hearts of Darkness chronicles the making of another of his films.
answers: Cannes (kahn) Film Festival [or Festival de Cannes; prompt on the International Film Festival]
answers_sanitized: Cannes (kahn) Film Festival [or Festival de Cannes; prompt on the International Film Festival]
